title: adenostyles alpina subsp. macrocephala
intro: Adenostyles alpina subsp. macrocephala, also known as Alpine Cottongrass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Adenostyles alpina subsp. macrocephala can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
